title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the New York Mets 6-2, achieving their seventh consecutive win.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run, securing his 11th win of the season.
Anthony Seigler's two-run homer and travel delays highlighted Boston's notable resilience and offensive performance.

title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez criticized reporter Greg Bedard's claim that some within the Patriots view him as not physical enough.
Gonzalez, a leading NFL cornerback, is in talks for a contract extension ahead of the 2026 season.
Patriots' 2026 secondary features Gonzalez, new addition Kevin Byard, Marcus Jones, and Carlton Davis.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox have the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft, scheduled for two days before the All-Star break.
Experts predict Boston might choose outfielder Chase Brunson from TCU or pitcher Taylor Rabe from Ole Miss.
Other potential picks include third baseman Bo Lowrance, Cameron Flukey, a right-handed pitcher, and outfielder Zion Rose.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Little House on the Prairie' adaptation is now streaming on Netflix, featuring Crosby Fitzgerald and Alice Halsey.
'The Long Walk,' a dystopian Stephen King adaptation, is available on HBO Max featuring Cooper Hoffman and David Jonsson.
'The Five-Star Weekend,' starring Jennifer Garner as a celebrity chef, is streaming on Peacock with an emotional storyline.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela was named to the 2026 MLB All-Star team, replacing injured Aaron Judge.
Rafaela joins three other Red Sox players, increasing Boston's All-Star representation to four.
Through 87 games, Rafaela has a .283 average, 8 home runs, and 39 RBIs with a .768 OPS.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship on July 11 in Seabrook, New Hampshire.
Geoffrey Esper, world No. 3 competitive eater, holds chicken wing record: 281 wings in 10 minutes.
Event will determine winner by weight of cleaned wings, not number consumed, in 10-minute timeframe.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the final World Cup match held in Boston, drawing a crowd of 63,811.
French players Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé scored to secure the victory, despite Morocco's strong first half performance.
Boston successfully hosted the 2026 World Cup, generating global publicity and attracting enthusiastic fan groups like Scotland's Tartan Army.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola of Gloucester faces felony charges after allegedly using a WD-40-soaked rag in a murder plot.
Marianna DiMercurio claims Palazzola intended to drive them off a bridge due to a $160,000 debt.
Palazzola is indicted on several charges, including attempted murder, and faces two civil lawsuits.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Coco Fernandez became France's national soccer team's barber after a chance WhatsApp message during their stay in Boston.
Fernandez has provided haircuts for Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé as France uses Boston as a World Cup base.
The Mobar owner also cut hair for Morocco's national team, including Achraf Hakimi, amid tight security measures.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Boston Red Sox faced a 17-hour flight delay leaving Chicago for their series against the New York Mets.
Red Sox eventually departed Chicago at 3 p.m. ET Friday and arrived in New York before the rescheduled 7:50 p.m. game.
Travel delays were due to a broken tug and a cockpit light issue, causing an overnight stay in Chicago.
